battery.description: Batteries should be taken to designated collection points for recycling or safe disposal. Do not throw into regular trash.
battery.environmental_impact: Batteries contain toxic chemicals that can pollute the environment if not disposed properly.
battery.tips: Store old batteries in a sealed container and bring to authorized collection centers.

biological.description: Biological waste includes food scraps, leaves, and other organic materials.
biological.environmental_impact: Without proper treatment, biological waste can cause odors and attract insects.
biological.tips: Separate biological waste for composting or bring to organic waste collection points.

brown-glass.description: Brown glass bottles typically used for beer and some beverages.
brown-glass.environmental_impact: Glass doesn't biodegrade but can be fully recycled.
brown-glass.tips: Rinse thoroughly and sort by color before bringing to collection point.

cardboard.description: Cardboard boxes and rigid paperboard products.
cardboard.environmental_impact: Cardboard can be recycled but needs to be sorted and kept dry.
cardboard.tips: Flatten boxes and keep dry before bringing to collection center.

clothes.description: Old or unused clothing and textiles.
clothes.environmental_impact: Clothes don't decompose quickly and can be harmful if incinerated.
clothes.tips: Donate to charity organizations or textile recycling centers.

green-glass.environmental_impact: Green glass can be recycled but needs to be sorted separately by color.
green-glass.tips: Rinse clean and sort by color before recycling.

metal.description: Metal products such as aluminum cans and steel items.
metal.environmental_impact: Metals can be recycled many times without losing quality.
metal.tips: Rinse clean and compress to save space.

paper.description: Printed paper, writing paper and other paper products.
paper.environmental_impact: Paper can be recycled but needs to be sorted and kept dry.
paper.tips: Don't crumple and keep dry before bringing to collection center.

plastic.description: Plastic products such as water bottles and plastic bags.
plastic.environmental_impact: Plastic takes hundreds of years to decompose and causes significant environmental damage.
plastic.tips: Rinse clean and sort by plastic code before recycling.

shoes.description: Old or damaged shoes and footwear.
shoes.environmental_impact: Shoes typically contain many difficult-to-decompose materials.
shoes.tips: Donate if still wearable or send to textile recycling centers.

trash.environmental_impact: Pollutes the environment and takes up landfill space.
trash.tips: Minimize usage and sort properly to reduce trash.

white-glass.environmental_impact: Clear glass can be recycled but needs to be sorted separately by color.
white-glass.tips: Rinse clean and sort by color before recycling.
